pairs.question: What was the designation of the 1999 East India Cyclone according to the India Meteorological Department (IMD)?
pairs.answer: The 1999 East India Cyclone was designated as BOB 05 by the India Meteorological Department (IMD).

pairs.question: On what date did the 1999 East India Cyclone make landfall on the Odisha coastline?
pairs.answer: The 1999 East India Cyclone made landfall on the Odisha coastline near Gopalpur beach at around 8am on October 17, 1999.

pairs.question: How many fatalities were directly attributed to the 1999 East India Cyclone?
pairs.answer: The 1999 East India Cyclone was responsible for at least 180 fatalities.

pairs.question: What was the peak intensity of the 1999 East India Cyclone in terms of wind speed?
pairs.answer: The 1999 East India Cyclone reached its peak intensity of 140 mph (220 km/h) on October 17, 1999.

pairs.question: Describe the path and intensification of the 1999 East India Cyclone from its formation to dissipation.
pairs.answer: The cyclone began as an area of low pressure located 220 nautical miles northwest of the Andaman Islands on October 15. It intensified and received its first advisory as Tropical Storm 04B by 2100Z on the same day. Moving west-northwest at 8–12 knots, it turned northward on October 17 and underwent explosive intensification to reach peak intensity of 140 mph. It made landfall near Gopalpur at around 8am on October 17 and weakened due to land interaction, dissipating by October 19.

pairs.question: What actions were taken in response to the 1999 East India Cyclone?
pairs.answer: The Prime Minister of India requested immediate distribution of relief supplies to the affected region following the cyclone's impact.

pairs.question: How did the 1999 East India Cyclone compare to other cyclones in the 1999 North Indian Ocean cyclone season?
pairs.answer: The 1999 East India Cyclone was the second strongest, deadliest, and costliest cyclone of the 1999 North Indian Ocean cyclone season.
